title: Job Openings at Jaza Energy
content:
Jaza is a solar energy start-up working to power Sub-Saharan Africa. We build solar-powered shops which charge batteries customers use to power their homes. In 2022, we achieved over 1.5 million battery swaps, providing approximately 186,000 people access to clean, affordable energy. We employ women from each community (known as Jaza Stars) to run the service, empowering our team to become leaders, entrepreneurs and drivers of economic growth within their communities. We’ve already successfully entered the Nigerian market, piloted several sites here and will grow to hundreds of locations over the next year. To support this expansion, we’re planning to hire over 200 people over the next 6 months.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



1.) Junior Hub Manager

Job Location: Abeokuta, Ogun
Employment Type: Contract
Job Function: Execution
Workplace Type: Onsite

Job Role


	The Junior Hub Manager is the second layer of the front-line team directly managing the launch of new Hubs/Shops and supporting growth toward Hub profitability.
	A Junior Hub Manager has an entrepreneurial mindset that drives new ideas to have a successful new Hub launch and enforces brilliant basics on Hub Operational Standards 
	A Junior Hub Manager has to demonstrate leadership capabilities by supporting jaza stars on best practices that help towards hub growth. Junior Hub Manager has to have the ability to recruit, train and coach other Jaza stars in his/her cohort.
	They will be responsible for managing Hub's growth which is increasing the number of active customers per respective Hub, Hub profitability and Jaza stars coaching at her respective cohort. 


Roles and Responsibilities


	Support Hub growth through customer acquisition initiatives to have an average of 130 active customers L7. Conduct door-to-door sales with Jaza stars at your respective cohort to grow the number of active customers towards 130 L7. 
	Managing customer retention by proactively following up with churned customers and support revenue growth at the Hub level through the implementation of membership renewal and changing customers' adaptation from pay-per-swap to memberships to have an average of 1M monthly recurring revenue and >$2.8 ARPU 
	Own Hub profitability and support Jaza stars on best practices to grow the Hub revenue emittance balancing 
	100% compliance with lost and missing packs metrics 
	Coaching Jaza stars best practices that work to have a profitable Hub on Sales (Sharpening sales pitch - removing customers blocks, closing sales & customer registration/signup) 
	Coaching Jaza stars on Customer service - (Proper customer and product education, Proper language and tone to use, answering questions, and resolving/ reporting complaints on time and leading by example on the Jaza behaviours and compliance with the SOPs. 
	Coaching Jaza stars on inventory management (Following packs scanning schedule, following up on missing packs at customers, following Pack in Pack out process, packs rotation, reporting defective packs at the hub and identifying tempered packs. 
	Flag any dilemma which limits Hub growth/ reliable service to the regional coordinator. 
	Own jaza stars recruitment process from advertisement, interviews and Jaza stars training. 
	Attend Hyper care meetings, providing clear action plans toward hubs growth, sharing the progress feedback and flag dilemmas.


Qualifications


	Bachelor Degree or its equivalent in sales, Administration, Marketing or any other related discipline. 
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in Sales, Customer Relations, Entrepreneurship or other related fields. 
	Strong leadership capabilities with experience leading, managing or training a team previously. 
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate with people from diverse backgrounds. 
	Data-driven and can make analytical decisions. 
	You are excited about our mission of increasing access to energy and employment for women.


Application Closing Date: 28th April, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

2.)  Construction Engineer

Job Location: Oyo
Employment Type: Contract
Job Function: Expansion
Designation: Acquisition Engineer
Workplace Type: Onsite

Position Overview


	The Construction Engineer (Expansion) is the owner of the process of service location acquisition,renovations and installation in our expansion process into new sites.
	The Construction Engineer findssuitable service locations, negotiates, leases, procure equipment, renovates, installs, audits and signs offon those allocated sites according to our company’s predefined standards.


Accountabilities


	Own the acquisition, construction, renovation and installation process for approved sitesidentified in our expansion pipeline by ensuring 100% compliance with the acquisition,construction, renovations and installation process.
	Own and manage sourcing and contracting of vendors for all activities of construction,renovation of a shop or installation of a hub, including management of materials, tools andequipment used.
	Own and audit the work completion per allocated site according to defined standards.


Roles & Responsibilities


	Assess approved sites and identify suitable services locations, shops / plots of land for leasingthat meet our defined standards.
	Manage the lease or acquisition of shops or plot of land that meet our service location criteria.
	Assess the acquired service location and identify preparations or renovations requirements.
	Manage the sourcing of appropriate furniture producing factories/contractors that meet the setcriteria to produce Jaza furniture and install hubs across allocated sites.
	Request, procure, take delivery and document required items, tools and equipment needed forthe completion of the work.
	Ensure availability of furniture across allocated regions/sites undergoing Expansion to ensurethere are no delays in the set timelines.
	Monitor renovations and construction needs and constantly reports need for tools, materials andequipment with reference to the set targets across allocated locations undergoing Expansion
	Manage quality checklists and ensure that they are adhered to in the process of acquisition,renovation and construction.
	Work effectively with engineers, supply chain, and other construction teams, ensuring seamless integrationbetween different parts of the project.
	Document and report milestones reached during this process.


Qualifications


	A relevant Degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering or a related field.
	Deep Understanding of the overall construction process, including shop or land negotiation, leasing and acquisition, renovation, site preparation, furnishing and foundation work, and structural assembly.
	Ability to interpret blueprints and design specifications, especially for our customized hubs, ensuring theymeet both the structural and electrical needs for solar installations.
	Ability to think critically and come up with solutions for unique or complex construction challenges,particularly when working with already built shops.
	Good data collection, analytical, and reporting skills.
	Ability to commit to high level quality standards.
	Clear communication skills to interact with suppliers, contractors, and other stakeholders and explain complex technical concepts in simple terms.
	Ability to work independently, interact with people of different backgrounds, cultures, languages and totravel frequently to remote locations.


Application Closing Date: 9th April, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

3.) Full Stack Software Engineer (Web)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time (Onsite)

Position Overview


	We’re looking for a strong backend-focused fullstack engineer to own internal tooling, APIs, and workflow automation across Ops, Finance, and Device Data.
	You’ll work primarily in a Laravel/PHP ecosystem, with exposure to Python and JavaScript, and be responsible for stabilising and extending our core backend systems: payment logic, integrations, data contracts, and automation workflows.
	This role is a mix of deep backend work (70–80%) and pragmatic fullstack tasks (20–30%) to keep internal tools fast, reliable, and easy to use.
	You’ll be joining a small, highly-autonomous backend team and will have an immediate impact on reducing bottlenecks, improving reliability, and unlocking new workflows for a product that takes Solar Energy to Africa’s most rural areas.”


Roles And Responsibilities
The Full stack Software Engineer responsibilities include, but are not limited to, the following:
Internal Tools Development:


	Build and maintain internal web tools used by Ops, Finance, Device teams, and other internal stakeholders.
	Translate manual workflows into robust, auditable tools and dashboards.
	Migrate some Python logic into the main Laravel system.


API Layer:


	Design, build and maintain RESTful APIs in Laravel/PHP.
	Ensure secure, well-documented, and backwards-compatible APIs for internal and external consumers for scale migration System Integrations
	Own integrations with payment providers, telcos, CRMs, analytics, and other  third-party systems.
	Monitor and fix integration failures and edge cases, especially those tied to money and critical ops workflows.


Workflow Automation Engine:


	Implement and maintain automation logic for recurring operational workflows(collections, device events, notifications, settlement flows, etc.).
	Replace brittle manual processes with reliable automated pipelines.


Data Contracts & Reliability:


	Define and maintain data contracts between services and teams.
	Ensure data correctness for financial and operational reporting, including well-tested and observable code.


Qualifications
Required:


	3+ - 5+ years of experience in full-stack web development
	Fluent in English, both written and spoken, with excellent communication skills for working across international teams
	Strong proficiency in PHP and the Laravel framework, including Eloquent ORM, middleware, and service containers
	Solid understanding of REST APIs
	Relational databases (PostgreSQL)
	Experience with Laravel's queue system, job processing, and message queuing systems such as RabbitMQ
	Working knowledge of Redis for caching, session management, and queue handling
	Experience building and maintaining internal tools and automation workflows, including scripting repetitive tasks and scheduled jobs
	Hands-on experience with Livewire for building dynamic interfaces
	Proficiency in Alpine.js and comfort with JavaScript for internal UIs
	Strong CSS skills with Tailwind CSS
	Able to read and modify Python scripts
	Intermediate DevOps familiarity, including Docker, environments, logs, and basic CI/CD
	Familiarity with DigitalOcean infrastructure, including Droplets, managed databases, and Spaces
	Experience with version control using Git and GitLab


Preferred
Experience with:


	Payment providers, mobile money, or other FinTech-style integrations
	Telco, IoT, or device data integrations
	Event-driven architectures (queues, pub/sub) and workflow engines are used for designing systems where components communicate through events.
	Particle console


Prior Experience In:


	Distributed teams across multiple African markets
	Energy, infrastructure, or other operationally intensive industries
	Experience mentoring junior engineers or leading small squads


Familiarity With:


	Basic infrastructure-as-code
	Monitoring/alerting tools (e.g., Sentry, Prometheus/Grafana)


Must have skills:


	Experience: 5+ Years of Exp Full-Stack African Markets
	Backend: PHP Laravel Eloquent ORM REST APIs
	Frontend: Livewire Alpine.js Tailwind CSS
	Data & Queuing: PostgreSQL Redis RabbitMQ/Queues
	DevOps/Infra: GitLab Docker DigitalOcean
	Soft Skills: Fluent English International Teams
	Scripting: Python (Reading/Modifying).


Application Closing Date: 28th April, 2026.

Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at TECO Group
content:
TECO is a longstanding Nigerian Engineering firm, founded in 1980. We excel in delivering tailored engineering solutions to a wide range of industries throughout Nigeria and the rest of Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Sales Officer - South South

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a smart, confident, and results-driven Sales Officer based in the South-South axis of Nigeria to drive revenue growth and expand our customer base in the oil and gas sector.
	The ideal candidate should be passionate about sales, persuasive, and able to build strong relationships with clients. You must be goal-oriented, proactive, and willing to go the extra mile to meet and exceed sales targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and develop new business opportunities and clients in the oil and gas sector.
	Promote and sell the company’s products and services to prospective customers.
	Build and maintain strong client relationships.
	Meet and exceed monthly and quarterly sales targets.
	Prepare and deliver sales presentations and proposals.
	Follow up on leads, inquiries, and client feedback.
	Maintain accurate records of sales activities and customer interactions.
	Provide regular sales reports and market feedback.
	Collaborate with internal teams to ensure customer satisfaction and service delivery.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field.
	1–3 years of experience in sales or business development.
	Experience in engineering, technical services, oil and gas, or B2B sales is an added advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent communication and negotiation skills
	Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills
	Confidence and ability to persuade clients
	Target-driven and results-oriented mindset
	Good customer service skills
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
	Good time management and organizational skills


What We’re Looking For:


	Someone who is energetic, self-motivated, and passionate about sales
	A candidate who is resilient and not easily discouraged
	Someone who pays attention to detail and follows through on tasks
	A professional with a positive attitude and a willingness to learn
	The person will be working remotely, to cover the south-south axis as we do not have a physical office there.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Sales Officer - South South" as the subject of the email.

 

2.) Finance Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Finance Manager will be responsible for financial planning, budgeting, financial reporting, cost control, tax compliance, and internal financial controls across the organisation.
	The role will also supportproject financial monitoring, ensuring that fabrication and installation projects remain financially viable and properly accounted for.
	The successful candidate will work closely with management, project teams, procurement, and operationsto ensure strong financial governance and profitability.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Management & Reporting:


	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements.
	Maintain accurate general ledger, financial records, and accounting systems.
	Provide financial analysis and reportsto management for strategic decisions.
	Ensure compliance with accounting standards and financial regulations.


Budgeting & Cost Control:


	Develop and monitorannual budgets and departmental budgets.
	Trackproject costs and profitability for palm oil mill fabrication,installation and electrical projects.
	Implement systems tocontrol operational and project costs.
	Monitor cash flow and financial performance.


Tax & Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with tax regulations (PAYE, VAT, Withholding Tax, Company Income Tax).
	Coordinate statutory filings and regulatory reporting.
	Liaise with external auditors, tax consultants, and regulatory authorities.


Financial Controls & Risk Management:


	Establish and enforceinternal financial control systems.
	Monitorprocurement and payment processesto ensure financial discipline.
	Identify and mitigate financial risks affecting operations.


Project Financial Oversight:


	Work closely withproject managers and operations teamsto monitor project budgets.
	Reviewproject cost estimates and financial feasibility.
	Ensure properfinancial documentation for project expenditures.


Team Leadership:


	Supervise and guideaccounting and finance staff.
	Support training and development within the finance function.
	Promote a culture offinancial accountability across the organisation.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related discipline.


Professional Qualification:


	ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent professional certification is required.


Experience


	Minimum 5–8 years of accounting/finance experience.
	Experience in engineering, manufacturing, construction, or project-based organisations will be an advantage.


Skills & Competencies


	Strong financial analysis and reporting skills
	Knowledge of tax laws and statutory compliance
	Budgeting and cost control expertise
	Strong Excel and accounting software proficiency
	Excellent leadership and communication skills
	This role is part of our gender balance initiative; therefore, applications from qualified female professionals will be prioritized.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Project Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Project Manager is responsible for planning, coordinating, and delivering engineering and fabrication projects from initiation to completion.
	The role ensures projects are executed safely, on time, within budget, and to required quality standards, while coordinating cross-functional teams including engineering, procurement, production, and site installation.


Key Responsibilities


	Plan, coordinate, and manage assigned projects from kick-off to final handover.
	Develop detailed project plans, schedules, timelines, and resource requirements.
	Coordinate with mechanical, electrical, procurement, production, finance, and QA teams to ensure smooth execution.
	Monitor project progress against milestones, budgets, and quality standards.
	Ensure timely procurement of materials and equipment in collaboration with procurement.
	Supervise site activities, including fabrication, installation, and commissioning.
	Risk Assessment and Mitigation—Identify project risks early and implement mitigation plans.
	Manage project budgets, track costs, and control variations.
	Prepare and present regular project status reports to management and clients.
	Ensure compliance with safety, quality, and regulatory requirements at all stages.
	Stakeholders Facing/Management—Serve as the primary point of contact for clients on project execution matters.
	Manage contractors, vendors and site personnel effectively.
	Resolve project issues, delays, and conflicts promptly.
	Ensure proper documentation, as-built drawings, and project close-out reports.
	Support continuous improvement of project execution processes.
	Oversee Bids & Tenders, RFQ, RFP processes.
	Local and Foreign OEM facing.
	Contract Management, SLAs within Nigeria and West Africa.
	Good knowledge of MS Project and Primavera P6.
	Project Documentation: Scope of Work, Project Contracts, Work Execution Plan, Budget Plan, WBS, Reports, RAMs, Operations and Maintenance Manuals etc.


Requirements


	Degree in Engineering (Mechanical Preferably) or related field
	Minimum 6 years of project management experience in engineering/fabrication/Construction projects
	Strong coordination, leadership, and problem-solving skills.


Salary
N400,000 - N600,000 per month. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

4.) Project Officer (Mechanical Engineering)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Mechanical Engineering / Engineering Services

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Project Officer to support the planning, coordination, and execution of engineering projects within our mechanical engineering operations.
	The ideal candidate will be fluent in communication, passionate about delivering quality results, and capable of managing multiple project tasks efficiently.
	The role requires someone who can work collaboratively with technical teams, ensure project timelines are met, and maintain strong attention to detail throughout the project lifecycle.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in project planning, scheduling, and execution of mechanical engineering projects.
	Coordinate with engineers, technicians, contractors, and vendors to ensure smooth project implementation.
	Monitor project timelines, milestones, deliverables, and budgets.
	Prepare and maintain project documentation, reports, and progress updates.
	Support procurement and logistics coordination for project materials and equipment.
	Ensure compliance with engineering standards, safety regulations, and company policies.
	Track project performance and escalate potential risks or delays.
	Facilitate communication between internal teams and external stakeholders.
	Participate in site visits, inspections, and project meetings when required.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Agricultural Engineering, Engineering Management, Project Management, or related field.
	2–5 years of experience in project coordination or engineering project management.
	Experience working in a mechanical engineering, construction, manufacturing, or technical services environment is an advantage.


Technical Skills:


	Project Management
	Engineering Project Coordination
	Project Scheduling
	Technical Documentation
	Project Reporting
	Engineering Operations Support
	Procurement Coordination
	Risk Management
	Microsoft Excel
	Microsoft Project / Project Management Software
	Engineering Documentation
	Project Lifecycle Management.


Interpersonal & Professional Skills:


	Excellent communication skills (written and verbal)
	Fluent English communication
	Strong attention to detail
	Problem-solving and analytical thinking
	Time management and organizational skills
	Team collaboration and stakeholder management
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	High level of professionalism and accountability
	Passion for engineering projects and continuous improvement
	Adaptability and proactive attitude.


Salary
N200,000 - N400,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
